trajectory.description: Observation: Baseline ResNet50 achieved test balanced accuracy 0.22 with large gap to validation (0.56). Rare classes (bird,coyote,squirrel) 0% recall. Analysis: Simple architecture insufficient for domain shift. Need stronger representation (CLIP/DINO) and robust domain generalization. Decision: Upgrade to CLIP ViT-B/16 backbone (pre-downloaded), add mixup + RandAugment augmentation, use differential learning rates, train 15 epochs to measure improvement and iteration time.

trajectory.description: Observation: CLIP ViT-B/16 with 15 epochs achieved test balanced accuracy 0.5636, but validation balanced accuracy reached 0.877, indicating severe domain overfitting. Rare classes (bird 0.259, squirrel 0.091) are still very low. Analysis: Simple augmentation (MixUp alone) insufficient to close domain gap; class imbalance hurts rare species. Decision: Implement domain adversarial training (DANN) using provided domain labels, add weighted cross-entropy loss, heavier RandAugment, dropout, and train longer (30 epochs) with early stopping. Monitor improvement via eval submission.

Observation: test balanced accuracy improved to 0.602 with DANN (val BA 0.858). However, still large domain gap, rare classes (squirrel 0.09, bird 0.31) weak. Analysis: DANN helps but not enough; model overfits source domain distribution. Decision: Add SWAD (stochastic weight averaging across late epochs in high-BA valley), focal loss for hard examples, stronger augmentation (RandAugment magnitude 12, ColorJitter, grayscale), and increase dropout to 0.4. Keep CLIP ViT-B/16 backbone (proven). Train 40 epochs with early stopping. This should improve generalization without doubling model size.

trajectory.description: Observation: SWAD best.pt gave test BA 0.47, far below previous 0.602. The training curve shows val BA up to 0.859 but test dropped heavily. Analysis: Likely SWAD averaging corrupted weights or checkpoint loading mismatch. Decision: Inspect train.py and checkpoint contents to understand failure, then revert to simpler DANN approach and explore DINOv2 backbone.

Observation: iter 5 SWAD test BA 0.47, worse than previous 0.602; iter 6 diagnostic command failed with syntax error. The SWAD approach likely overfit or introduced noise. Need to revert to simpler DANN approach with proven backbone and try DINOv2 for better domain-invariant features. Decision: rewrite model.py to add DINOv2-base (pre-downloaded), create new train.py with class-balanced sampling, focal loss (gamma=1.0), MixUp, DANN, early stopping, no SWAD, train for 20 epochs; update predict.py to support DINOv2 normalization. This should recover performance and potentially improve.

trajectory.description: Observation: DINOv2+DANN training completed (20 epochs, val BA 0.848), iteration took 1394s (23 min). No test eval yet. Previous SWAD approach gave 0.47 test BA. Analysis: need test performance to compare with earlier CLIP-based DANN (0.602) and decide if DINOv2 improves domain generalization. Decision: submit eval on best.pt to measure current test balanced accuracy and plan further steps. Remaining time: 1.19h, iterations: 23.

Observation: iter 8 eval gave test BA 0.506 with DINOv2+DANN, below best 0.602 (CLIP+DANN). Bird improved to 0.63 but many other classes dropped. Analysis: CLIP features transfer better for most classes; DINOv2 overfits source domains. Need to close domain gap with stronger regularization and domain alignment. Decision: revert to CLIP ViT-B/16 backbone (proven), add CORAL loss (covariance alignment between domain features), stronger augmentation (RandAugment mag=12, higher mixup), label smoothing, and train 30 epochs. Time budget allows 2-3 training runs.

trajectory.command: mkdir -p logs/iter_11 && export RAB_ITERATION=11 && python train.py --backbone clip_vitb16 --use_dann --coral_lambda 0.0 --focal_gamma 1.5 --label_smoothing 0.1 --dropout 0.4 --mixup_alpha 0.4 --randaug_magnitude 15 --domain_lambda 0.4 --epochs 20 --early_stop_patience 8 --seed 123
trajectory.description: Observation: Iter 10 eval of CLIP+DANN+CORAL (epoch 27) achieved test balanced accuracy 0.555, below previous best 0.602. Validation BA peaked at 0.868, but domain gap still ~0.31. Training curve shows domain loss explosion after epoch 27, indicating instability. CORAL loss may be counterproductive. Analysis: Need to revert to simpler DANN without CORAL, strengthen focal loss (gamma=1.5), increase augmentation (RandAug mag=15), and train a quick 20-epoch run (~18 min) to test within remaining 35 min. Decision: Retrain CLIP+DANN (no CORAL) with improved hyperparameters, then submit eval. If score improves, may use TTA or ensemble of top checkpoints for final boost.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iter 11 training completed (CLIP+DANN, no CORAL, improved hyperparams) in 1320s, but test performance unknown. Remaining time ~12.8 min. Analysis: Must evaluate the new model quickly. The best checkpoint (best.pt) should reflect the highest validation balanced accuracy from this run. Decision: Submit eval on checkpoints/best.pt immediately to measure test BA. Based on result, either finalize or attempt TTA/ensemble within remaining time.

Observation: Iter 12 eval showed test BA 0.546, below best 0.602. Remaining time ~12 min, iterations 18. Cannot train new model. Analysis: Best score so far came from iter 3 CLIP+DANN (0.602). We have preserved epoch checkpoints up to 40, likely including the model from that run. Test-time augmentation (TTA) could boost rare-class recall and push score higher without training. Decision: Identify the best checkpoint from iter 3 logs (highest val BA), then create a predict.py that performs 10-crop TTA (5 crops + flips) to improve generalization, submit eval. This is our only viable strategy to exceed 0.602 within remaining time.
